subject: Physics
topics: Semiconductor devices: p-n junction diode, Zener diode as voltage regulator
Transistor as amplifier and switch: CE configuration, current gain
Logic gates: AND, OR, NOT, NAND, NOR - truth tables and Boolean expressions
Communication systems: modulation, bandwidth, propagation of EM waves
Nuclei: nuclear binding energy, mass defect and packing fraction
Radioactivity: alpha, beta, gamma decay laws and half-life calculations
Nuclear fission and fusion: Q-value calculation and chain reaction
Dual nature of matter: de Broglie wavelength and Davisson-Germer experiment
Photoelectric effect: Einstein's equation, stopping potential and threshold frequency
Atoms: Bohr model limitations, hydrogen spectrum series
Ray Optics: refraction at spherical surfaces, lens maker's equation derivation
Optical instruments: compound microscope and astronomical telescope
Wave Optics: Huygen's principle and proof of laws of reflection and refraction
Single slit diffraction: central maximum width and secondary maxima positions
Polarisation: Brewster's angle and Malus's law
Electromagnetic Induction: Lenz's law applications and motional EMF
Self-inductance and mutual inductance: energy stored in inductor
AC circuits: LCR series resonance, quality factor and power factor
Transformers: step-up and step-down, energy losses
Electrostatics: potential energy of system of charges, equipotential surfaces
Capacitors: combination in series and parallel, energy stored
Dielectrics: polarisation and electric susceptibility
Current Electricity: Kirchhoff's laws applications to Wheatstone bridge
Potentiometer: comparison of EMFs and internal resistance measurement
Magnetism: magnetic field on axis of current loop, Ampere's circuital law
Moving Coil Galvanometer: conversion to ammeter and voltmeter
Mechanical Properties: Poisson's ratio and elastic energy stored in wire
Fluid Mechanics: Bernoulli's theorem applications and Torricelli's theorem
Kinetic Theory: degrees of freedom, law of equipartition of energy
Simple Harmonic Motion: combination of SHMs, Lissajous figures
Waves: stationary waves in strings and organ pipes, resonance conditions
